name: Parchment-Wrapped Potato Parcels
recipeIngredient: 1 small Red bell pepper (cut into 1-inch squares)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut two 15-inch squares of kitchen parchment.
2. For each packet, place half the ham on the center of a parchment square.
3. Top the ham with half the potato slices, overlapping them slightly.
4. Add half the remaining vegetables (zucchini, squash, and bell pepper) on top.
5. Sprinkle half the remaining ingredients (lemon juice, dried basil, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper) over the vegetables.
6. Fold two sides of the parchment over the ham and vegetables in a letter fashion.
7. Grasp the two shorter open ends and pull them up to meet at the top.
8. Fold down the open ends twice to seal the parcel securely.
9. Repeat steps 2-8 for the second packet.
10. Place the parcels in a single layer in a shallow microwave-safe dish.
11. Microwave on HIGH for 7 minutes.
12. Rotate the parcels halfway, then microwave for another 7 minutes.
13. Let stand for 3 minutes; open one parcel and check that the potatoes are tender.
14. If the potatoes are not cooked through, rewrap and microwave for a little longer.
NOTE: Microwave cooking times may vary based on your microwave's wattage. Adjust accordingly.
Menu: Tomato Soup, French Bread, Toasted Pound Cake with Fresh Seasonal Fruit
Nutritional Information Per Serving: 250 calories; 4 g fat; 25 mg cholesterol; 980 mg sodium; 30 g carbohydrate; 4 g fiber; 23 g protein.
